name: Coastal and inland villages of the Sierra de Cabrera — circular route through the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park
name_original: Pueblos costeros e interiores de la Sierra de Cabrera — circular por el Parque Natural Cabo de Gata-Níjar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Carboneras traverse a landscape characterized by volcanic formations and semi-desert terrain, offering varied elevation changes. The region features a rugged coastline with views of the Mediterranean Sea and extends inland to hilly areas. Cyclists can experience both coastal roads and more challenging climbs through the volcanic hills. This diverse environment provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Carboneras The most popular road cycling route is Coastal and inland villages of the Sierra de Cabrera — circular route through the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, a 53.0 miles (85.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Cabrera. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is The coastal villages of Cabo de Gata - circular through the Cabo de Gata-Nijar Natural Park, a difficult 59.2 miles (95.2 km) path. It offers extensive coastal views within the natural park. Local road cyclists also love the La Granatilla Viewpoint – Agua Amarga Beach loop from Sendero Rio Alias, a 93.3 miles (150.1 km) trail leading through coastal and volcanic landscapes, often completed in about 7 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Carboneras is defined by volcanic formations, rugged coastline, and semi-desert lands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Carboneras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 9 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carboneras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Carboneras?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes around Carboneras, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes for beginners in Carboneras?
A: Yes, Carboneras offers routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40340077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carboneras Promenade loop from Carboneras&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 11.7 km ride with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed introduction to the area's cycling.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Carboneras?
A: The terrain around Carboneras is diverse, characterized by volcanic formations and semi-desert landscapes. You'll find a rugged coastline with stunning Mediterranean Sea views, as well as hilly inland areas. Routes can range from coastal stretches to more challenging climbs through volcanic hills.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40359705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granatilla Viewpoint – Agua Amarga Beach loop from Sendero Rio Alias&lt;/a&gt; cover over 150 km with more than 2,000 meters of elevation gain, traversing coastal and volcanic landscapes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987145588/coastal-and-inland-villages-of-the-sierra-de-cabrera-circular-route-through-the-cabo-de-gata-nijar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal and inland villages of the Sierra de Cabrera — circular route through the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant climbs.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views. You can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Granatilla viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides fantastic panoramas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesa Roldán Lighthouse and Tower&lt;/a&gt; is another magnificent viewpoint perched on a volcanic plateau, offering stunning sea and landscape vistas. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pirulico Tower&lt;/a&gt; also offers coastal views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987145588/coastal-and-inland-villages-of-the-sierra-de-cabrera-circular-route-through-the-cabo-de-gata-nijar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal and inland villages of the Sierra de Cabrera — circular route through the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986722947/the-coastal-villages-of-cabo-de-gata-circular-through-the-cabo-de-gata-nijar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The coastal villages of Cabo de Gata - circular through the Cabo de Gata-Nijar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, both offering extensive exploration of the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Carboneras?
A: Carboneras, located in Almería, Spain, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. The semi-desert climate means mild winters and hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with pleasant temperatures and less intense sun, making for comfortable rides. Even winter can be suitable, offering a good escape from colder climates elsewhere.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Carboneras and the surrounding villages offer various cafes and restaurants. The Paseo Marítimo in Carboneras itself is lined with cafes. Routes that pass through villages like Agua Amarga or other coastal and inland settlements within the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park will provide opportunities for refreshment stops.

Q: What makes the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park special for road cyclists?
A: The Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park is a protected area known for its unique volcanic formations, rugged coastline, and semi-desert landscapes. Cyclists can enjoy routes with breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea, hidden coves, and dramatic geological features. The park's diverse environment provides both challenging climbs and scenic coastal rides.

Q: Are there any famous beaches accessible or visible from the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its stunning beaches. While cycling, you might catch glimpses of or pass near beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4037171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Muertos Beach&lt;/a&gt;, consistently ranked among Spain's most beautiful. Other notable beaches in the area include Algarrobico Beach and the tranquil coves within the natural park.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Carboneras?
A: Road cycling routes in Carboneras generally feature mostly well-paved surfaces. However, given the volcanic and semi-desert nature of the region, some roads might have varying conditions, especially on less-trafficked routes or those leading to more remote areas within the natural park. It's always advisable to check route details and be prepared for varied surfaces.
tips: A beautiful town located at the northern tip of Cabo de Gata, a white village with a spectacular beach and turquoise waters. Surrounded by the typical mountains of the area.
Great climb to the Arabian Cabreras. An Englishman bought the hill and built a little Morocco. I thought we were in the Atlas Mountains.

The beautiful white Moorish village of Mojácar is perched on a cliff, 2 km from the sea, and from afar, you have a magnificent view of this typical Andalusian village. The village is part of the Mojácar to San Juan de los Terreros coast. From Mojácar, you have stunning views of the sea and the Tabernas desert.

One of the most spectacular viewpoints of the Cabo de Gata Natural Park, where you can not only see the protected space but also the old thermal power plant and the Carboneras desalination plant in the distance.
